chunks.text: A driver's license is a symbol of adult status in many countries.
The freedom to drive one's own car and go anywhere.
It is an aspiration for many young people.
But driving comes with great responsibility.
A car is a convenient tool, but with a single wrong judgment
it can become a weapon that takes a life.
The result of a wrong operation can be fatal.
In recent years, self - driving technology has advanced rapidly.
The age is coming when AI operates many parts of driving
and the human operator is placed in a passive position.
There are five levels of autonomous driving.
Currently, most cars are around the initial level 2.
They are at the stage of assisting some functions
such as lane keeping and automatic braking.
Fully autonomous driving, level 5, is still under development.
Whether AI can make smarter decisions than a human in complex traffic and a traffic jam is still open to debate.
In the case of an accident, who is responsible?
The driver, the carmaker, or the AI developer?
Current law has not caught up with this new phase.
If autonomous driving spreads, the number of traffic deaths is expected to decrease.
Accidents caused by human inattention, fatigue, or drunk driving do not happen with AI.
But at the same time, many taxi and truck drivers may lose their jobs.
Technological advance is inevitable, but we must keep thinking about how society adapts.
